phases.description: Next.js 16 (App Router) + React 19 + Tailwind 3 scaffold, design tokens and global styles, and the single-page marketing surface (Hero, Pricing, HowItWorks, Conditions, Clinicians, FAQ, Nav, Footer). Complete.
phases.label: PHASE 1
phases.name: Foundation

phases.description: Compliance pages and content rules for LegitScript telehealth certification: /about, /privacy (HIPAA Privacy Policy + Notice of Privacy Practices), /terms (telehealth disclosure + refunds), FDA-status disclosures on Conditions cards, and the Apothem-to-Apoth rebrand with corporate-structure alignment (Apoth Health LLC + MD Integrations). Complete (PR #1 merged).
phases.label: PHASE 2
phases.name: LegitScript Compliance

phases.description: Resolve the unresolved DESIGN.md tokens (OKLCH clay/sage/cream palette values, display serif + humanist sans font selection), document the component primitives now that they exist in code, and run a WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility audit against the finalized palette and type scale.
phases.label: PHASE 3
phases.name: Design System
phases.summary: Finalize design tokens, document components, a11y audit

phases.description: AWS foundation that everything downstream depends on: VPC + RDS + App Runner via CDK, Secrets Manager, observability skeleton, Postgres baseline schema with Drizzle, KMS envelope encryption, audit-log hash chain, webhook ingestion plumbing (SQS + worker + idempotency), and consent-versioning data model. The BAA boundary is established here.
phases.label: PHASE 4
phases.name: Infrastructure
phases.summary: AWS foundation: CDK, RDS, secrets, observability, webhook plumbing, audit log, KMS

phases.description: Build out patient authentication: auth provider setup (Clerk recommended for Next.js email/social login + session management), sign-up/sign-in UI aligned to the design system, session management with protected routes, and a patient account/profile model. Introduces server-side logic to a previously static site.
phases.label: PHASE 4
phases.name: Auth Flow

phases.description: Replace the /get-started stub with the real patient intake flow: test framework setup, intake route structure, error/404/loading routes, state-availability logic, per-condition clinical eligibility screening, and the per-condition intake form UI. Honors the "no card charged before clinical confirmation" promise.
phases.label: PHASE 5
phases.name: Intake Flow

phases.description: 503A compounding pharmacy partner selection, BAA execution, and validation that fulfillment events flow through MDI's e-prescribe passthrough. Direct pharmacy API integration is deferred until a concrete gap (real-time tracking, custom shipping, inventory visibility) is named.
phases.label: PHASE 10
phases.name: Pharmacy Fulfillment
phases.summary: Pharmacy partner BAA + validate MDI passthrough; direct integration deferred

phases.description: Stripe payment processor integration: account and SDK setup, visit checkout/payment flow gated on passed clinical eligibility, subscription and auto-renewal billing (as disclosed in Terms), webhook handling for payment events, and refunds/cancellation handling matching the Terms refunds matrix.
phases.label: PHASE 6
phases.name: Payments

phases.description: LegitScript-grade patient identity verification via a hosted KYC vendor (Persona recommended, pending BAA). Placement: signup → consents → KYC → intake → payment → MDI handoff. Declined KYC produces no Stripe customer and no MDI patient. KYC is an identity-of-record control distinct from MDI's clinical driver-license capture.
phases.label: PHASE 7
phases.name: Identity Verification
phases.summary: Persona hosted Inquiry flow + webhook + decline UX, sits before payment

phases.description: The clinical-handoff integration: OAuth client-credentials token cache, single-entrypoint MDI HTTP client, patient + case creation, 40+ webhook event handlers (HMAC + bearer verified, idempotent, SQS-queued), case-status mirroring, embedded URL helpers for sensitive sub-flows (driver-license, face-photo, intake questionnaire, clinician messaging, file uploads), MDI subscription lifecycle for refills, 418 maintenance handling, and the 6-hour reconciliation job. Highest-discovery-risk phase per the architecture plan.
phases.label: PHASE 8
phases.name: MDIntegrations
phases.summary: MDI client + 40+ webhooks + dashboard + embedded URLs + reconciler

phases.description: Real data and external sign-off required before LegitScript submission (deprioritized — mostly waiting on legal/corporate input): healthcare attorney review of /privacy and /terms, real contact info, mailing address, NPI, pharmacy partner disclosure, and finalized tiered pricing.
phases.label: PHASE 7
phases.name: Launch Blockers

phases.description: Production-grade reconciliation (Stripe ↔ MDI drift), audit-chain integrity verifier, drift dashboards, admin audit timeline tool, dunning + cross-system cancellation sync, retention/deletion policy implementation, penetration test, and the LegitScript submission package with controls-evidence runbooks.
phases.label: PHASE 12
phases.name: Hardening & LegitScript Prep
phases.summary: Reconcilers, audit chain, dashboards, LegitScript submission package, pen test

phases.description: Production readiness and launch: SEO and metadata assets (robots, sitemap, favicon, OG image), analytics and cookie consent backing the privacy policy's cookie disclosure, production domain config with HTTPS/WHOIS, and the production deployment.
phases.label: PHASE 8
phases.name: Deploy
phases.summary: SEO assets, consent, domain, production deploy
